content.text: How can I ensure that nodes designated for uninferable calls don't inadvertently call non-callable methods or properties?
What happens if one of the return values from a decorated method is conditionally callable?

The code requires that all resulting return values from the function execution must be considered callable when evaluated during inference to pass. If not, it flags a 'not-callable' error message and breaks.
Thanks for clarifying how those methods interact with call checks!
